name: cirq
description: Quantum computing framework for building, simulating, optimizing, and executing quantum circuits. Use this skill when working with quantum algorithms, quantum circuit design, quantum simulation (noiseless or noisy), running on quantum hardware (Google, IonQ, AQT, Pasqal), circuit optimization and compilation, noise modeling and characterization, or quantum experiments and benchmarking (VQE, QAOA, QPE, randomized benchmarking).
capabilities: Build quantum circuits with gates, measurements, and parameterized operations
Simulate circuits using state vector, density matrix, and stabilizer methods
Run circuits on quantum hardware from Google, IonQ, Azure, AQT, and Pasqal
Model and simulate noise including depolarizing, amplitude damping, and thermal noise
Optimize circuits through gate decomposition, merging, and qubit routing
Implement quantum algorithms including VQE, QAOA, and Quantum Phase Estimation
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing markdown guides and code examples for the Cirq quantum computing library. All 336 static findings are false positives - the scanner detected markdown code formatting backticks, quantum computing terminology, and documentation URLs, misinterpreting them as security issues. No executable code, network endpoints, or file operations exist.
valueStatement: Quantum computing requires specialized tools for circuit design and simulation. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for Cirq, enabling you to create quantum algorithms, run noisy simulations, and execute on real quantum hardware from multiple providers.
limitations: Requires Python and Cirq library installation (uv pip install cirq)
Hardware access requires separate accounts and API credentials
Simulation scales exponentially with qubit count (practical limit 25-30 qubits)
Does not provide quantum hardware itself, only integration interfaces

useCases.title: Research quantum algorithms
useCases.description: Design and test new quantum algorithms including variational methods, optimization, and phase estimation

useCases.title: Learn quantum computing
useCases.description: Understand quantum circuit concepts through simulation and experiment with fundamental quantum phenomena

useCases.title: Develop quantum software
useCases.description: Build and compile quantum circuits for deployment on multiple quantum computing platforms

promptTemplates.title: Basic circuit
promptTemplates.scenario: Create a simple Bell state circuit
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Bell state preparation circuit using Cirq with two qubits, Hadamard and CNOT gates, and measurement

promptTemplates.title: Noisy simulation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to add depolarizing noise to a quantum circuit and simulate it using DensityMatrixSimulator

promptTemplates.title: Parameterized circuits
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a parameterized quantum circuit with sympy symbols and demonstrate parameter sweeps using cirq.Linspace

promptTemplates.title: Hardware execution
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to compile and run a circuit on Google Quantum AI hardware with device validation and qubit selection

bestPractices: Start with simulator testing before running on expensive quantum hardware
Use density matrix simulation only when needed for mixed states or noise
Select best qubits using calibration data to improve hardware results
Optimize circuits for target hardware gateset before submission
antiPatterns: Running unvalidated circuits directly on hardware without simulator testing
Using density matrix simulation for pure state circuits (exponentially slower)
Ignoring device constraints and qubit connectivity when designing circuits
Running simulations with too many qubits without considering memory limits

faq.question: What quantum hardware providers are supported?
faq.answer: Cirq supports Google Quantum AI, IonQ, Azure Quantum, AQT, and Pasqal. Each requires separate API credentials and accounts.

faq.question: How many qubits can I simulate?
faq.answer: Exact simulation scales as 2^n in memory. Practical limits are typically 25-30 qubits (several GB of RAM). Clifford circuits can simulate more efficiently.

faq.question: How do I install Cirq?
faq.answer: Run 'uv pip install cirq' for the core library. Add provider-specific packages like cirq-google, cirq-ionq, or cirq-aqt for hardware access.

faq.question: Is my quantum circuit data safe?
faq.answer: Cirq runs locally. Hardware providers may log submitted circuits for service operation. Review each provider's privacy policy for details.

faq.question: Why is my simulation slow?
faq.answer: State vector simulation is faster than density matrix. Reduce qubits or use CliffordSimulator for Clifford-only circuits. Monitor memory usage.

faq.question: How does Cirq compare to Qiskit?
faq.answer: Cirq focuses on circuit-level design with deep hardware integration. Qiskit offers higher-level abstractions and a larger ecosystem. Choose based on your use case.
